AlgoSec Diagnostic Framework

AlgoSec Diagnostic Framework

Section titled “AlgoSec Diagnostic Framework”

Support Cockpit is the current interaction model for the Starlight publisher.

Live baseline

algosec-lab on algosec running Rocky Linux 8.10 (Green Obsidian).

Observed scope

263 services, 56 listeners, 114 config checkpoints, 109 log checkpoints.

First response

Section titled “First response”

How to use this site

  • Confirm the customer appliance hostname, OS family, and any visible build markers against the lab baseline.
  • Check whether the top AlgoSec services are active and enabled in the customer environment.
  • Compare Apache-routed endpoints and local listener ports before moving into deeper service-specific diagnosis.
  • Use the recorded config and log checkpoints for the top services before inferring broader platform issues.

Why this shape

  • Quick jump stays visible while you read lower command content.
  • Each checkpoint stays collapsed until you need it.
  • The JSON baseline stays canonical. This page is only a render.

Playbooks

Section titled “Playbooks”

ui-and-proxy

ASMS UI is downservices: httpd.service, keycloak.service, ms-metro.service

Check whether the host is under storage, inode, memory, or CPU pressure before blaming the ASMS UI path. Confirm Apache/HTTPD can answer local UI traffic and still route toward the auth and app branches.

core-aff

FireFlow Backendpaths: aff-boot; services: aff-boot.service, algosec-dfs.service, postgresql.service

Confirm aff-boot.service is active and /FireFlow/api or /aff/api still proxy to localhost:1989. Confirm postgresql.service is active before treating FireFlow symptoms as application-only.

microservice-platform

Microservice Platformpaths: ms-batch-application, ms-bflow, ms-cloudlicensing, ms-configuration; services: algobot-microsoft-teams.service, algobot-slack.service, algobot-webex-teams.service, algosec-ms.service, elastic-ssh-tunnel.service, httpd.service

Confirm algosec-ms.service completed successfully and the top ms-* services are active. Compare the routed microservice paths first: ms-batch-application, ms-bflow, ms-cloudlicensing.

messaging-and-data

Messaging and Dataservices: activemq.service, postgresql.service

Confirm activemq.service is active and listener 61616 is present. Confirm postgresql.service is active and local database access is healthy.

Symptom lookup

Section titled “Symptom lookup”
ASMS UI is down

Check whether the host is under storage, inode, memory, or CPU pressure before blaming the ASMS UI path.

Use ASMS UI is down.
ASMS login page not loading

Check whether the host is under storage, inode, memory, or CPU pressure before blaming the ASMS UI path.

Use ASMS UI is down.
FireFlow action failing

Confirm aff-boot.service is active and /FireFlow/api or /aff/api still proxy to localhost:1989.

Use FireFlow Backend.
FireFlow API error

Confirm aff-boot.service is active and /FireFlow/api or /aff/api still proxy to localhost:1989.

Use FireFlow Backend.
Feature failing behind UI

Confirm algosec-ms.service completed successfully and the top ms-* services are active.

Use Microservice Platform.
Specific microservice path failing

Confirm algosec-ms.service completed successfully and the top ms-* services are active.

Use Microservice Platform.
Job stuck or not progressing

Confirm activemq.service is active and listener 61616 is present.

Use Messaging and Data.
Data-backed action failing

Confirm activemq.service is active and listener 61616 is present.

Use Messaging and Data.
Next
ASMS UI is down
